channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In a small Lebanese town, grief and fear follow the Michigan synagogue attack
item.description: The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect who attacked a synagogue in Michigan lost family members in an Israeli strike in Lebanon on March 5. Relatives and neighbors in his hometown share their views on his actions.

(Image credit: JOSEPH EID)
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: President Trump orders a Marine rapid response force to Middle East
item.description: NPR's Jane Arraf reports on developments in the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Jane Arraf reports on developments in the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: A Game Challenges Californians To Find A Working Payphone
item.description: When was the last time you saw a payphone? For most people it's probably been a while. In California, there are more than 2000 working payphones. A game challenges the public to find them.
item.content:encoded: When was the last time you saw a payphone? For most people it's probably been a while. In California, there are more than 2000 working payphones. A game challenges the public to find them.
item.dc:creator: Vincent Acovino

item.title: Fixing the toxic work culture of restaurants
item.description: Host Adrian Ma speaks with restaurant industry veterans Kenji Lopez-Alt and Hannah Selinger about ways to fix the toxic work culture in many restaurant kitchens.
item.content:encoded: Host Adrian Ma speaks with restaurant industry veterans Kenji Lopez-Alt and Hannah Selinger about ways to fix the toxic work culture in many restaurant kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Paramount, Warner Bros. and regulators
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Columbia Law professor Tim Wu who makes the case for what he sees as the weak spots in the Paramount Warner-Bros. merger.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Columbia Law professor Tim Wu who makes the case for what he sees as the weak spots in the Paramount Warner-Bros. merger.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: From golf greens to the Grammys: Leven Kali's unusual musical journey
item.description: Leven Kali brings the funk in his new album LK99. The Netherlands-born artist shares his inspiration and drive with NPR's Adrian Ma.
item.content:encoded: Leven Kali brings the funk in his new album LK99. The Netherlands-born artist shares his inspiration and drive with NPR's Adrian Ma.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Your Child's Pediatrician May Be Able To Provide Literacy Screenings
item.description: Sara Bode of Nationwide Children's Hospital in Ohio speaks with NPR's Emily Kwong about why many pediatric centers have started screening kids for literacy skills.
item.content:encoded: Sara Bode of Nationwide Children's Hospital in Ohio speaks with NPR's Emily Kwong about why many pediatric centers have started screening kids for literacy skills.
item.dc:creator: Emily Kwong

item.title: Unlike past eras, anti-Muslim GOP rhetoric draws little pushback from party leaders
item.description: A growing chorus of Republicans in Congress have embraced rhetoric against Muslims and sharia law. But unlike in past years, their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.
item.content:encoded: A growing chorus of Republicans in Congress have embraced rhetoric against Muslims and sharia law. But unlike in past years, their remarks have faced little public pushback from leadership.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: States pass laws against health insurers' prior authorization
item.description: More than 30 states have passed laws trying to rein in health insurance companies second guessing doctors, a policy called prior authorization.
item.content:encoded: More than 30 states have passed laws trying to rein in health insurance companies second guessing doctors, a policy called prior authorization.
item.dc:creator: April Dembosky

item.title: New Music Friday: Johnny Blue Skies and the Dark Clouds
item.description: The country singer-songwriter formerly known as Sturgill Simpson has a new album out under his current stage name, Johnny Blue Skies and the Dark Clouds.
item.content:encoded: The country singer-songwriter formerly known as Sturgill Simpson has a new album out under his current stage name, Johnny Blue Skies and the Dark Clouds.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt
